qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Element name injection via unvalidated createElement allows XML structure manipulation and XSS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns createElement not validating element names against XML QName production rules. The advisory concerns createDocumentType not validating internalSubset/publicId/systemId fields during DocumentType serialization. These are different APIs (createElement vs createDocumentType), different validation gaps (tag name vs DOCTYPE fields), and different serialization paths in the XMLSerializer.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes unvalidated DocumentType serialization via createDocumentType (internalSubset/publicId/systemId fields emitted verbatim without escaping). The only qualifying finding (element-name-injection) describes unvalidated element names via createElement. These are different APIs, different root causes, and different vulnerable code paths.
evidence: Advisory root cause: createDocumentType stores internalSubset/publicId/systemId without validation; XMLSerializer emits DocumentType nodes verbatim without escaping
Finding root cause: createElement assigns tagName to node.nodeName/node.tagName/node.localName without QName validation
Finding code locations: lib/dom.js:2181-2192 (createElement); Advisory code locations: DOMImplementation.createDocumentType and DocumentType serialization
The two vulnerabilities share only surface-level similarity: both are in @xmldom/xmldom and both relate to unvalidated input in XML serialization
